economizeSuggestions.title: How to read Total cluster on OpenShift
economizeSuggestions.detail: Nodes / vCPU / memory are Kafka broker + controller pod requests (KafkaNodePool replicas), not OpenShift worker or infra node SKUs. Pack those pods onto dedicated worker nodes with affinity/taints; do not schedule Kafka on control-plane/infra nodes.
economizeSuggestions.source: Streams for Apache Kafka 3.2 — Deploying on OpenShift (KafkaNodePool roles broker|controller)

economizeSuggestions.title: Subscription cores track broker capacity
economizeSuggestions.detail: Reported subscription figure is 16 (corePairs). Controllers (3 × 4 vCPU) and Cluster/Entity Operator cores are outside the broker licensing line used here. Align reporting with your Red Hat entitlement (core pairs vs failover-excluded).
economizeSuggestions.source: Streams release notes — subscription limits / operator cores; product methodology

economizeSuggestions.title: Revisit overstated consumer groups
economizeSuggestions.detail: Binding constraint is network with 12 groups. Over-counting independent groups inflates netRead and can force extra brokers (now 4). Use peak concurrent independent groups, not topic count.

economizeSuggestions.title: Right-size workers; never move Kafka to infra
economizeSuggestions.detail: Economize OpenShift by packing broker/controller pods onto fewer large workers (or dedicated machine sets), not by placing them on infra/control-plane. Production guidance: separate broker and controller NodePools; dual-role only for non-prod.
economizeSuggestions.source: Streams for Apache Kafka 3.2 Overview — Node pools / role separation

economizeSuggestions.title: Match storage class to cost/latency
economizeSuggestions.detail: Per-broker PVC size already includes capacity headroom. Prefer LSO/local NVMe when latency and cost matter; use ODF/Ceph RBD when you need shared block HA. Over-provisioned storage class is a common silent cost.
economizeSuggestions.source: Streams deploying guide — persistent storage / JBOD

notes: Separate KafkaNodePool for broker and controller roles (KRaft).
